# Aion Programming Language

> Aion is the world's first AI-native programming language designed specifically for Large Language Models and agentic workflows. It features a Minimally Tokenized Syntax, Gradual Verification via a Formal Query Language (FQL), AST-First Contextualization, and hardware-agnostic autotuning via an MLIR backend.

## Core Documentation
- [Aion Philosophy and Architecture](/docs/architecture.md)
- [Memory Management](/docs/memory.md)
- [Standard Library](/docs/stdlib.md)

# Aion Programming Language

> Aion is the world's first AI-native programming language designed specifically for Large Language Models and agentic workflows. It features a Minimally Tokenized Syntax, Gradual Verification via a Formal Query Language (FQL), AST-First Contextualization, and hardware-agnostic autotuning via an MLIR backend.

## Core Documentation
- [Aion Philosophy and Architecture](/docs/architecture.md): Core design pillars, pipeline orchestration, and transition from human-centric to AI-native coding.
- [Formal Syntax Specification](/docs/syntax.md): Complete ISO/IEC 14977 EBNF grammar optimized for token efficiency (no visual whitespace formatting).
- [Formal Semantics](/docs/semantics.md): Axiomatic semantics for the `#intent` block verification and Structural Operational Semantics (SOS) for LLVM IR lowering.
- [Environment Setup](/docs/getting-started.md): Installing the Aion compiler, configuring LLM API keys, and setting up the VS Code dual-syntax extension.

## Language Reference
- [Intent-Based Programming](/docs/intent.md): How to use mandatory `#intent` blocks and non-computable specifications.
- [Memory Management](/docs/memory.md): AI-guided ownership, gradual verification, and sandboxed garbage collection fallbacks.
- [Type System](/docs/types.md): Predictive strong typing using the `infer` keyword and struct-based MLIR lowering.
- [Validation and Error Handling](/docs/validation.md): Implementing native validation loops, strict null safety, and `verify` blocks.

## Optional
- [Standard Library](/docs/stdlib.md): Built-in arrays, primitives, and external HTTP modules.
- [Interoperability](/docs/interop.md): Zero-copy Python integration and C++ MLIR linking.
- [Code Examples](/docs/examples.md): Side-by-side comparisons of Aion vs. Python and Rust for typical AI orchestration tasks.
